file_name: Magenta Therapeutics, Inc. - Master Development and Manufacturing Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.18 "Specifications" means the requirements of all Applicable Laws, the master batch record, current standard operating procedures and the procedures, process parameters, analytical tests and other attributes and written specifications for the Product attached hereto as part of a Project Plan, which the Parties agree are necessary for the manufacture and release of the Product for use in clinical trials. The Parties recognize that specifications for Product for a specific Project Plan are likely to change during the term of this Agreement, and the Parties agree to act in good faith and reasonably to effect such changes as may be required. Copies of such Specifications, as amended, shall be maintained by both Parties, and shall be incorporated into this Agreement and the Quality Agreement (as defined below).
perturbation.changed_text: 1.18 "Specifications" means the initial requirements outlined at the start of a Project Plan, including the master batch record, initial standard operating procedures, and preliminary analytical tests for the Product. These Specifications are considered fixed unless explicitly amended through a formal written amendment to this Agreement. The initial Specifications document will be attached to each Project Plan for reference. Any deviations from these initial Specifications, even if required for improvements or regulatory compliance, are considered a breach of contract unless formally approved via a written amendment.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction because it creates an ambiguity concerning the modification of specifications. The original text allowed for changes to specifications during the term of the agreement, and the parties should act in good faith to effect such changes as may be required. On the contrary, the changed text states the specifications are considered fixed unless approved via a written amendment. This alteration contradicts the original flexibility, potentially leading to disputes about implementing necessary changes to the product specifications, particularly in Section 5.4, which mentions deliverables must conform to specifications, as the nature of specifications is now ambigious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Deliverables. Each deliverable (including Product) developed or produced in connection with a Project Plan and this Agreement shall conform to the Specifications. The Development Work, as described in the Project Plan, shall conform to the Development Specifications. Bachem shall warrant compliance with the agreed acceptance criteria together with the results as reported on the Certificate of Analysis in conjunction with the analytical methods at the time of the release of Product. In no event shall Bachem be liable for any defects that could not have been detected by Bachem with the analytical test methods in operation at the date of product release. For reasons of clarity, the Parties acknowledge and agree that it shall remain solely the responsibility and liability of Magenta to determine the suitability of the Product for any intended or specific use of the Product. Bachem makes no expressed or implied guarantees, warranties or undertakings as to the use of the Product for an intended or specific purpose or use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text regarding 'Specifications' introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text allows for changes to specifications during the agreement term, promoting good faith adjustments. Conversely, the changed text fixes specifications unless formally amended, contradicting the earlier flexibility. This directly impacts Section 5.4, which requires deliverables to conform to Specifications. The ambiguity created by the changed text, making specifications effectively fixed unless formally amended, directly clashes with the original allowance for adjustments, leading to uncertainty about the deliverables and a potential breach of contract.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Services. Bachem shall provide the Services in accordance with each Project Plan. Bachem will perform all Services in accordance with this Agreement and the agreed upon Specifications. All Products shall be packaged, labeled and shipped in accordance with this Agreement, the applicable Project Plan and all Applicable Laws. Bachem and its employees and agents have, and will continue to have, the knowledge, experience, facilities, equipment and skill to provide, and will provide, the Services in a professional and timely manner. Services will conform to consistently high standards of workmanship and the specifications applicable to each Project Plan.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Services. Bachem shall provide the Services in accordance with each Project Plan. Bachem will endeavor to perform all Services in general alignment with this Agreement and the general intent of the Specifications. All Products shall be packaged, labeled and shipped following general industry practices and in substantial compliance with Applicable Laws. Bachem and its employees and agents will make reasonable efforts to provide the Services in a generally competent manner, using the facilities and equipment available. Services will generally aim to meet acceptable industry standards of workmanship applicable to each Project Plan, subject to resource availability.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by weakening the obligations on Bachem regarding the performance of services and adherence to specifications and applicable laws. The original text stated that Bachem 'shall provide' services in accordance with the Project Plan and 'will perform' all services in accordance with the agreement and agreed-upon specifications. In contrast, the modified text states that Bachem 'will endeavor to perform' the services, suggesting a non-binding commitment, and the agreement should be made by the 'general intent of the specifications'. This change contradicts the original language that demanded strict adherence, leading to uncertainty about the required level of compliance, especially in Section 6.2(a), which grants Magenta the right to reject a Product, because the standard that the product should meet is now ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Acceptance and Rejection of Products. (a) Promptly following receipt of Product, Magenta shall have the right but not the obligation to test such Product to determine compliance with the Specifications. Magenta shall have [***] after receipt of the Product to notify Bachem in writing of any rejection of Product based on a sufficiently documented claim that the Product fails to meet the Specifications. In the event that Magenta does not inform Bachem within the [***] period that the Product does not meet the Specifications, Magenta shall be deemed to have accepted the Product. If there is no dispute between the Parties over a claim that the Product fails to meet the Specifications, Bachem shall (i) replace or (ii) with Magenta's prior written consent, reprocess or rework the rejected Product within an agreed upon time frame, after the notice of such rejection, and in any case as soon as reasonably possible after receiving such notice, provided that Magenta shall, at Bachem's expense, provide to Bachem sufficient quantities of supplies required to be supplied by Magenta under the relevant Project Plan, at no additional cost to Magenta (including transportation costs), and Bachem shall make arrangements with Magenta for the return or disposal of any rejected Product, such return shipping or disposal charges to be paid by Bachem. In the event of a discrepancy between Magenta's and Bachem's test results such that one Party's test results fall within relevant Specifications and the other Party's test results fall outside the relevant Specifications, or there exists a dispute between the Parties over the extent to which such failure is due to acts or omissions of Bachem, the Parties shall cause an independent GMP laboratory or appropriate experts promptly to review records, test data and perform comparative tests and/or analyses on samples of the alleged defective Product. Such independent laboratory shall be mutually agreed upon by the Parties. The independent laboratory's results shall be in writing and shall be final and binding save for manifest error. Unless otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing, the costs associated with such testing and review shall be borne by the Party against whom the independent laboratory rules.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens Bachem's obligations regarding the performance of services and adherence to specifications and applicable laws by using terms like 'endeavor to perform', 'general alignment', 'general intent', and 'reasonable efforts'. This directly contradicts Section 6.2(a), which grants Magenta the right to reject a Product if it fails to meet the Specifications. The original text, which demanded strict adherence to the Specifications, is now replaced with a more ambiguous standard, making it unclear what constitutes a valid reason for rejection under Section 6.2(a). The phrase "fails to meet the Specifications" in 6.2(a) now lacks a clear standard for application.

perturbation.original_text: 11.2 By Bachem. Bachem shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Magenta, its Affiliates, directors, officers, employees and agents from and against Losses arising out of or in connection with: (i) any Product that does not meet the Specifications, (ii) Bachem's labeling or improper manufacturing, handling, use or storage of a Product, (iii) any gross negligence, willful misconduct or misrepresentation by Bachem or material breach by Bachem of this Agreement, or (iv) any Latent Defects in a Product, except to the extent that such Losses are attributable to the gross negligence or willful misconduct of or breach of this Agreement by Magenta.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.2 By Bachem. Bachem shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Magenta, its Affiliates, directors, officers, employees and agents from and against Losses directly resulting from: (i) any Product that demonstrably fails to meet critical Specifications impacting patient safety, (ii) Bachem's intentional mislabeling or gross negligence in manufacturing, handling, use or storage of a Product directly causing harm, (iii) any gross negligence, willful misconduct or intentional misrepresentation by Bachem directly and proximately causing Losses, or (iv) any Latent Defects in a Product directly attributable to Bachem's gross negligence in testing procedures, except to the extent that such Losses are attributable to the gross negligence or willful misconduct of or breach of this Agreement by Magenta.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a significant in-text contradiction by narrowing the scope of Bachem's indemnification obligations. Where the original text stated that Bachem 'shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless' Magenta from Losses arising out of several scenarios, the modified version limits indemnification to situations where Losses 'directly result' from more egregious conduct or failures, such as 'intentional mislabeling' or defects 'directly attributable to Bachem’s gross negligence'. This alteration creates ambiguity and weakens the protection afforded to Magenta, conflicting with the obligations in Section 5.4 which states that 'Each deliverable (including Product) developed or produced in connection with a Project Plan and this Agreement shall conform to the Specifications,' because if the product doesn't meet specifications but this does not 'directly results' into loss, there is an unbalance between the original contract and the liability in this case.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Deliverables. Each deliverable (including Product) developed or produced in connection with a Project Plan and this Agreement shall conform to the Specifications. The Development Work, as described in the Project Plan, shall conform to the Development Specifications. Bachem shall warrant compliance with the agreed acceptance criteria together with the results as reported on the Certificate of Analysis in conjunction with the analytical methods at the time of the release of Product. In no event shall Bachem be liable for any defects that could not have been detected by Bachem with the analytical test methods in operation at the date of product release. For reasons of clarity, the Parties acknowledge and agree that it shall remain solely the responsibility and liability of Magenta to determine the suitability of the Product for any intended or specific use of the Product. Bachem makes no expressed or implied guarantees, warranties or undertakings as to the use of the Product for an intended or specific purpose or use.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in section 11.2 significantly narrows Bachem's indemnification obligations to Losses 'directly resulting' from egregious conduct or failures, such as 'intentional mislabeling' or defects 'directly attributable to Bachem’s gross negligence.' This weakens the protection afforded to Magenta. This directly contradicts section 5.4, which states, 'Each deliverable (including Product) developed or produced in connection with a Project Plan and this Agreement shall conform to the Specifications.' If a product fails to meet specifications but does not 'directly result' in a loss due to Bachem's gross negligence, section 11.2, as modified, would not require indemnification, thus creating an unbalance between the original contract and the liability. This introduces an ambiguity and in-text contradiction because the original section 5.4 requires all products to conform to specifications, and if they don't, it's unclear how it interacts with the new indemnification restrictions in 11.2, making enforcement and liability ambiguous.
